title: Faithful Long-Document Scientific Summarization with Section-Aware Hierarchical Inference
paper_text:
Abstract:
Scientific papers are difficult to summarize because they are long and section-structured.

Introduction:
This paper studies scientific summarization and aims to preserve faithfulness in long-document settings.

Method:
The approach uses section-aware summarization, hierarchical synthesis, and claim-level evidence retrieval.

Results:
The system improves ROUGE-L by 2.4 points over a strong single-pass baseline and reduces unsupported claims in manual review.

Conclusion:
The method is practical for long scientific documents and improves interpretability through evidence support scoring.
